edge_hypothesis: stochastic_reversal template has a confirmed bug (line 174-175: short side checks k_now > 80 instead of k_prev > 80, making shorts nearly impossible). This means stochastic reversal has NEVER been properly tested — 0 trades across 17+ cycles is a bug artifact, not a signal failure. Stochastic K/D crossover in extreme zones is one of the most widely-used reversal signals in traditional trading. By implementing the correct symmetric logic via spec_rules (bypassing the buggy template), we can finally test whether stochastic reversals have edge in crypto. CHOP > 50 gates for ranging markets where mean-reversion works. This is the first proper test of Stochastic as an entry signal in our system.
target_regime: ranging (CHOP > 50 confirms range-bound market) where mean-reversion signals have historically worked (ranging PF > 1.0 for RSI, CCI)

variants.name: stoch_chop_reversal_tight_8to1
variants.description: Stochastic K/D crossover in extreme zones (K < 25 for long, K > 75 for short) under CHOP ranging gate. Tight zones = fewer but higher-conviction mean-reversion entries. Bypasses the buggy stochastic_reversal template with correct symmetric logic via spec_rules.

components.notes: STOCHk_14_3_3 / STOCHd_14_3_3 crossover in OS/OB zones — correct symmetric logic (bug fix of template)
CHOP_14_1_100 > 50 confirms ranging market where mean-reversion has proven edge

variants.name: stoch_chop_reversal_wide_7to1
variants.description: Stochastic K/D crossover with wider zones (K < 30 for long, K > 70 for short) for more trades. 7:1 R:R to capture shorter mean-reversion moves. Tests whether relaxing the OS/OB threshold increases trade count enough to compensate for lower conviction per trade.

components.notes: STOCHk_14_3_3 / STOCHd_14_3_3 crossover in wider OS/OB zones (30/70)

variants.name: stoch_ema200_reversal_8to1
variants.description: Stochastic K/D crossover in extreme zones with EMA_200 trend filter instead of CHOP gate. Long only above EMA_200 (uptrend pullback), short only below (downtrend rally). Tests stochastic reversal as a pullback entry in trends rather than mean-reversion in ranges — a fundamentally different use case that diversifies the hypothesis.

components.notes: STOCHk_14_3_3 / STOCHd_14_3_3 crossover in extreme zones — pullback entry signal
EMA_200 as trend direction filter — only trade pullbacks in the direction of the major trend
